paper_title: Mothman at SemEval-2024 Task 9: An Iterative System for Chain-of-Thought Prompt Optimization

key_features: Systematic evaluation of input data and model output using human performance as benchmark
Identification of difficult question types to inform prompt engineering
Model reasoning over all answer choices with explanations for both correct and incorrect choices

contributions: Novel approach for identifying reasoning challenges to optimize prompting
Prompt engineering method for sentence-based tasks
More consistent results suggesting less reliance on memorization

evaluation.approach: Combination of model reasoning with human evaluation
evaluation.focus: Identification of problematic questions in adversarial datasets

background.field: Question Answering (QA) in natural language processing
background.comparison: BrainTeaser questions require lateral thinking, unlike vertical thinking in CommonSenseQA and StrategyQA

data.generation: Incorrect choices created by handpicking entailments generated by COMET using incorrect premises
data.structure: Three unique answers per question plus a shared fourth option, 'None of above'

title: Mothman at SemEval-2024 Task 9: An Iterative System for Chain-of-Thought Prompt Optimization
